next.name: check
tip: Runs formatter, linter and import sorting to the requested files.

option.name: --assist-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the assist.

option.name: --changed
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been changed compared to your `defaultBranch` configuration will be linted.

option.name: --enforce-assist
tip: Allows enforcing assist, and make the CLI fail if some actions aren't applied.

option.name: --fix
tip: Alias for `--write`, writes safe fixes, formatting and import sorting

option.name: --format-with-errors
tip: Whether formatting should be allowed to proceed if a given file has syntax errors.

next.name: false
tip: Skip formatting files with syntax errors.

next.name: true
tip: Allow formatting files with syntax errors.

option.name: --formatter-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the formatter check.

option.name: --linter-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the linter check.

option.name: --only
tip: Run only the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --since
tip: Use this to specify the base branch to compare against when using the --changed flag.

option.name: --skip
tip: Skip the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --staged
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been staged will be linted.

option.name: --stdin-file-path
tip: Use this option when you want to format code piped from `stdin`.

option.name: --unsafe
tip: Apply unsafe fixes. Should be used with `--write` or `--fix`

option.name: --watch
tip: Enables the watch mode to re-run the check automatically when any non-excluded file in the workspace has changed.

option.name: --write
tip: Apply safe fixes, formatting and import sorting

next.name: ci
tip: Command to use in CI environments. Runs formatter, linter and import sorting.

option.name: --assist-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the assist.

option.name: --changed
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been changed compared to your `defaultBranch` configuration will be linted.

option.name: --enforce-assist
tip: Allows enforcing assist, and make the CLI fail if some actions aren't applied.

option.name: --format-with-errors
tip: Whether formatting should be allowed to proceed if a given file has syntax errors.

next.name: false
tip: Skip formatting files with syntax errors.

next.name: true
tip: Allow formatting files with syntax errors.

option.name: --formatter-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the formatter check.

option.name: --linter-enabled
tip: Allow enabling or disabling the linter check.

option.name: --only
tip: Run only the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --since
tip: Use this to specify the base branch to compare against when using the --changed flag.

option.name: --skip
tip: Skip the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --threads
tip: The number of threads to use. Useful for CI environments with limited resources.

next.name: clean
tip: Cleans the logs emitted by the daemon.

next.name: explain
tip: Shows documentation of various aspects of the CLI.

next.name: format
tip: Run the formatter on a set of files.

option.name: --changed
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been changed compared to your `defaultBranch` configuration will be formatted.

option.name: --fix
tip: Alias of `--write`, writes formatted files to a file system.

option.name: --since
tip: Use this to specify the base branch to compare against when using the --changed flag.

option.name: --staged
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been staged will be formatted.

option.name: --stdin-file-path
tip: Use this option when you want to format code piped from `stdin`.

option.name: --watch
tip: Enables the watch mode to re-run the format automatically when any non-excluded file in the workspace has changed.

option.name: --write
tip: Writes formatted files to a file system.

next.name: init
tip: Bootstraps a new biome project. Creates a configuration file with some defaults.

option.name: --jsonc
tip: Tells Biome to emit a `biome.jsonc` file.

next.name: lint
tip: Run various checks on a set of files.

option.name: --changed
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been changed compared to your `defaultBranch` configuration will be linted.

option.name: --only
tip: Run only the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --since
tip: Use this to specify the base branch to compare against when using the --changed flag.

option.name: --skip
tip: Skip the given lint rule, assist action, group of rules and actions, or domain.

option.name: --staged
tip: When set to true, only the files that have been staged will be linted.

option.name: --stdin-file-path
tip: Use this option when you want to format code piped from `stdin`.

option.name: --suppress
tip: Fixes lint rule violations with comment suppressions instead of using a rule code action.

option.name: --unsafe
tip: Apply unsafe fixes. Should be used with `--write` or `--fix`

option.name: --watch
tip: Enables the watch mode to re-run the lint automatically when any non-excluded file in the workspace has changed.

next.name: lsp-proxy
tip: Acts as a server for the Language Server Protocol over stdin/stdout.

option.name: --watcher-kind
tip: Controls how the Biome file watcher should behave.

option.name: --watcher-polling-interval
tip: The polling interval in milliseconds. Only applicable when using the polling watcher.

next.name: migrate
tip: Updates the configuration when there are breaking changes.

option.name: --fix
tip: Alias of `--write`, writes the new configuration file to disk

option.name: --write
tip: Writes the new configuration file to disk

next.name: eslint
tip: Find the ESLint configuration file and update the Biome's configuration file as a result.

option.name: --include-inspired
tip: Includes rules inspired from an eslint rule in the migration

option.name: --include-nursery
tip: Includes nursery rules in the migration

next.name: prettier
tip: Find `.prettierrc`/`prettier.json` and `.prettierignore`, and map the Prettier's configuration into Biome's configuration file.

option.name: --daemon-logs
tip: Prints the Biome daemon server logs

next.name: search
tip: EXPERIMENTAL: Searches for Grit patterns across a project.

option.name: --language
tip: The language to which the pattern applies.

option.name: --stdin-file-path
tip: Use this option when you want to search through code piped from `stdin`.

next.name: start
tip: Starts the Biome daemon server process.

option.name: --watcher-kind
tip: Controls how the Biome file watcher should behave.

option.name: --watcher-polling-interval
tip: The polling interval in milliseconds. Only applicable when using the polling watcher.

next.name: stop
tip: Stops the Biome daemon server process.

next.name: upgrade
tip: Upgrade Biome to the latest version.

next.name: version
tip: Shows the Biome version information and quit.

global_option.name: --colors
tip: Set the formatting mode for markup: `off` prints as plain text, `force` forces ANSI formatting.

next.name: force
tip: Force ANSI formatting even if the console is incompatible.

global_option.name: --config-path
tip: Set the file path to the configuration file, or the directory path to find `biome.json`.

global_option.name: --diagnostic-level
tip: The level of diagnostics to show. Default: info.

next.name: info
tip: Show info, warn, and error diagnostics.

global_option.name: --error-on-warnings
tip: Tell Biome to exit with an error code if some diagnostics emit warnings.

global_option.name: --log-file
tip: Optional path/file to redirect log messages to.

global_option.name: --log-kind
tip: What the log should look like. Default: pretty.

global_option.name: --log-level
tip: The level of logging. Default: none.

global_option.name: --log-path
tip: Allows changing the folder where logs are stored.

global_option.name: --log-prefix-name
tip: Prefix name for log files. Default: server.log.

global_option.name: --max-diagnostics
tip: Cap the amount of diagnostics displayed. Default: 20.

global_option.name: --no-errors-on-unmatched
tip: Silence errors that would be emitted in case no files were processed.

global_option.name: --reporter-file
tip: Write reporter output to a file.

global_option.name: --skip-parse-errors
tip: Skip over files containing syntax errors instead of emitting an error diagnostic.

global_option.name: --use-server
tip: Connect to a running instance of the Biome daemon server.

global_option.name: --verbose
tip: Print additional diagnostics, and some diagnostics show more information.
